games.headline: Bruins’ early burst holds up as Boston keeps rolling at home
games.brief: Boston’s home rink is turning into a full-on survival bunker, and this one fit the theme: an early punch, then 40 minutes of hanging on. Pittsburgh scored 42 seconds in, but the Bruins answered with two goals less than a minute apart and basically spent the rest of the night telling the Penguins, “good luck.” Jeremy Swayman was the closer, swallowing 34 shots to protect Boston’s 11th straight home win—exactly the kind of streak that keeps a wild-card chase alive even when the overall form has been wobbly lately.
bullets: Erik Karlsson scored just 42 seconds into the game before Boston responded with two goals in 50 seconds.
Marat Khusnutdinov and Casey Mittelstadt produced the Bruins’ quick first-period surge.
Jeremy Swayman made 34 saves to secure the win.
Boston extended its home winning streak to 11 games.
games.context: Charlie McAvoy’s nine-game point streak ended, a run that had put him alongside Ray Bourque among Bruins defensemen with the longest point streaks in the past 40 years.

games.headline: Sabres keep surging as Thompson’s gold-medal night turns into another win
games.brief: Buffalo’s surge is getting impossible to brush off, and it even came with a little Hollywood: Tage Thompson gets honored for Olympic gold, then goes out and scores anyway. The Sabres built a 3-0 cushion—Thompson’s team-leading 33rd was the headline moment—and then had to absorb Vegas’ push when the Knights struck twice in 1:37 to make it sweaty. But this is what “real” teams do when they’re rolling: they don’t flinch. Buffalo’s now 25-5-2 over its last 32, and Alex Lyon quietly keeps stacking wins (13-2 in his last 15 starts), while Vegas keeps sliding without Mark Stone in the lineup.
bullets: Tage Thompson scored his team-leading 33rd goal after a pregame Olympic ceremony.
Buffalo improved to 25-5-2 over its last 32 games.
Alex Lyon made 29 saves and improved to 13-2 in his last 15 starts.
Vegas lost its third straight regulation game.
games.context: Buffalo’s 35 wins through 61 games are its most at that point of a season since 2006-07.

games.headline: Devils hammer Panthers as Florida’s playoff math gets uglier
games.brief: This is the kind of loss that makes the standings feel heavier the next morning. New Jersey smoked Florida again, and the Panthers’ playoff math is getting uglier by the week—down 10 points from the final wild card with only 21 games left, and they’ve dropped three of four since the Olympics ended (all in regulation). Jack Hughes has come back from Milan handing out points like it’s his job (an assist in all four games), and New Jersey got meaningful depth pop too—Cody Glass’ 14th, Arseny Gritsyuk finishing a 2-on-1. For Florida, it’s not just the scoreline—it’s the creeping “is this really happening?” feeling.
bullets: Jack Hughes has an assist in four straight games since returning from the Olympics.
Dougie Hamilton recorded a goal and an assist.
Cody Glass scored his 14th goal of the season.
Florida has lost three of four games since the Olympic break.
The Panthers are now 10 points behind the final Eastern Conference wild-card spot.
games.context: Aleksander Barkov is expected to miss significant time with ACL/MCL damage, leaving Florida trying to stay alive in the playoff race without its captain.

games.headline: Utah hangs on in Washington after a bizarre go-ahead bounce
games.brief: Washington got a reminder of how thin the margin is when you’re living on the bubble—and also how cruel hockey can be when the puck starts doing circus tricks. Utah jumped ahead early, and then JJ Peterka’s go-ahead goal turned into pure chaos: a bounce off the post and goalie Logan Thompson, then Thompson’s glove knocked it in—Peterka’s 21st made it 3-1. The Caps did get both goals on the power play (a rare bright spot), but they couldn’t find the equalizer late, and now they’re chasing Boston for that last wild-card spot with the Bruins holding games in hand. If you’re Washington, this is exactly the kind of night that makes the trade deadline feel a lot more urgent.
bullets: Utah grabbed a 2-0 lead early in the game.
JJ Peterka scored his 21st goal on a bizarre bounce sequence.
Washington scored both of its goals on the power play.
The Capitals sit four points behind Boston for the final Eastern wild-card spot.
games.context: Washington is evaluating potential trade-deadline additions as it tries to remain in the playoff hunt.

games.headline: Blue Jackets steal one late with short-handed spark and third-period winner
games.brief: Columbus looked like a team running on fumes—second night of a back-to-back—right up until they didn’t. The Blue Jackets flipped the game in the third with the kind of “how did that just happen?” sequence that fans remember: Sean Monahan ties it short-handed (again—he did it the night before too), and then Damon Severson’s point shot through traffic becomes the winner. Even the goalie story had drama—Jet Greaves left for concussion protocol after getting clipped, then came back and finished it. Nashville’s day also included a roster shake-up: the Predators traded Michael McCarron before the game.
bullets: Sean Monahan tied the game with a short-handed goal early in the third period.
Monahan has short-handed goals in back-to-back games.
Damon Severson scored the game-winner at 6:07 of the third.
Jet Greaves made 20 saves after briefly leaving for concussion protocol.
games.context: Earlier in the day Nashville traded Michael McCarron to Minnesota for a 2028 second-round pick.

games.headline: Jets steal two points late as Scheifele finishes it in overtime
games.brief: Winnipeg is in one of those stretches where every game feels like it’s being decided in the last breath, and this one was a gut check. Down late, they pull the goalie, Cole Perfetti ties it with 38.6 seconds left, and then Mark Scheifele ends it in overtime—classic “steal two points” energy. That’s now four straight games going beyond regulation for the Jets, and they’ll take any kind of win when you’re opening a big homestand. Chicago got offense where it’s been getting offense—special teams—and Connor Bedard chipped in two assists, but they’re the ones left staring at the “we were seconds away” part.
bullets: Cole Perfetti tied the game with 38.6 seconds left in regulation.
Mark Scheifele scored the overtime winner at 2:06.
Winnipeg has now gone to overtime in four straight games.
Connor Bedard recorded two assists for Chicago.
games.context: The Jets opened a key eight-game homestand and banked two points in a game that nearly slipped away.
